context_blocks.title: Runtime context 2
context_blocks.text: work on teh second cli like

runtime_understanding.runtime_summary: This project is an AutoDocX service that scans a local Python project, builds a parsed/analyzed representation, generates an LLM-produced narrative "understanding", and then renders and stores developer-facing HTML documentation. It exposes a FastAPI backend (app.main + app.api.project_routes) with endpoints to scan projects, compare scans, generate understandings, and create HTML documents. There is a Streamlit UI (ui/streamlit_app.py) that acts as a frontend to call the backend, including a special sync-with-context flow that accepts ordered runtime/context blocks and screenshots. LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic/Claude, or Claude-compatible options) are used for narrative generation and optional vision-capable analysis of uploaded screenshots.

tooling_stack.purpose: Primary runtime language for backend services, CLI tooling, and Streamlit UI.
tooling_stack.evidence: Project contains many .py modules (project scan metadata shows 27 python modules) and classes (45) and the Streamlit UI file ui/streamlit_app.py.

tooling_stack.purpose: HTTP API server framework providing endpoints for scanning, comparing, generating understandings, and saving documents.
tooling_stack.evidence: Numerous routes in app.api.project_routes (e.g. /api/projects/documentation/sync, /api/projects/scan) and app.main registering routes (root, /health).

tooling_stack.purpose: ASGI server used to run the FastAPI backend during development.
tooling_stack.evidence: Screenshot 1 terminal shows the command and uvicorn logs: 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000'.

tooling_stack.purpose: Local web UI for interactive documentation sync, including uploading runtime context blocks and screenshots.
tooling_stack.evidence: ui/streamlit_app.py functions (normalize_api_url, sync_documentation_with_context, build_context_blocks_payload) and screenshot 2 showing 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and 'Local URL: http://localhost:8501'.

tooling_stack.purpose: Generate narrative 'understanding' of the code and optionally analyze screenshots (vision-capable LLM).
tooling_stack.evidence: Config helpers get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model in app.core.config and service classes app.services.llm.openai_provider and app.services.llm.anthropic_provider; project_understanding_service.generate_understanding and runtime_context_analyzer.analyze are called.

tooling_stack.purpose: Used by Streamlit UI to call FastAPI endpoints (scan/sync flows).
tooling_stack.evidence: ui.streamlit_app.parse_json_response, sync_documentation, and sync_documentation_with_context use requests.post/requests.get; code references REQUEST_TIMEOUT_SECONDS and building multipart form payloads.

tooling_stack.purpose: Persistent storage of scans, understandings, generated HTML documents and runtime assets (screenshots).
tooling_stack.evidence: Storage classes (ScanStorage, UnderstandingStorage, DocumentStorage) with docstrings indicating files are saved under workspace paths; create_required_directories in app.core.config creates these directories.

tooling_stack.purpose: Temporarily persist uploaded screenshot files before copying into document storage.
tooling_stack.evidence: Function _save_uploaded_screenshots_to_temp uses tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile and returns temporary file paths; _cleanup_temp_files removes them.

runtime_flow.title: Start backend and UI
runtime_flow.description: Run the FastAPI backend via Uvicorn and the Streamlit frontend. Uvicorn serves the API at a local port (example shows 127.0.0.1:8000). Streamlit runs the UI on a separate port (example shows localhost:8501).
runtime_flow.evidence: Screenshot 1: 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' and 'Uvicorn running on http://127.0.0.1:8000'; Screenshot 2: 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' and 'Local URL: http://localhost:8501'. Code: app.main and ui/streamlit_app.py exist in project scan.

runtime_flow.title: User initiates a documentation sync (with or without runtime context)
runtime_flow.description: The Streamlit UI or a direct API client calls the sync endpoints. The basic sync endpoint (/documentation/sync) accepts a project_path and performs a full scan+generate flow. The 'sync-with-context' endpoint accepts Form fields plus uploaded screenshot files and a JSON list of ordered context blocks.
runtime_flow.evidence: Routes found in app.api.project_routes: sync_project_documentation (POST /documentation/sync) and sync_project_documentation_with_context (POST /documentation/sync-with-context). ui.streamlit_app implements sync_documentation and sync_documentation_with_context and builds multipart payloads via build_context_blocks_payload.

runtime_flow.title: Project scanning and saving
runtime_flow.description: ProjectScanner scans the local project directory using PythonCodeParser and ProjectAnalyzer output; the scan result is saved by ScanStorage and returns storage info including a scan_id.
runtime_flow.evidence: sync endpoints call project_scanner.scan_project and scan_storage.save_scan. Code modules app.services.project_scanner and app.services.scan_storage describe scanning and storing under workspace/scans/<project_name>/<scan_id>.json.

runtime_flow.title: Runtime context processing (optional)
runtime_flow.description: If context blocks or screenshots are provided, uploaded files are saved to temporary files, DocumentStorage.prepare_runtime_assets copies them into project asset directories, and _build_runtime_context_from_blocks links each block to its correct screenshot by screenshot_index. Optionally a vision-capable LLM analyzes the runtime context (screenshots + notes) to create structured runtime_context data.
runtime_flow.evidence: Functions: _save_uploaded_screenshots_to_temp, document_storage.prepare_runtime_assets, _build_runtime_context_from_blocks, and _analyze_runtime_context_with_llm are present in app.api.project_routes. ui.streamlit_app builds payloads containing context_blocks_json and files (screenshots). User-supplied metadata lists two context blocks and two screenshots mapped by filenames.

runtime_flow.title: Generate LLM understanding
runtime_flow.description: The service obtains configured provider, API key and model, then calls ProjectUnderstandingService.generate_understanding with the stored scan (and optionally runtime_context). The generated 'understanding' JSON is saved by UnderstandingStorage and a new understanding_id is returned.
runtime_flow.evidence: sync endpoints call _generate_and_save_understanding which uses get_llm_provider/get_llm_api_key/get_llm_model and project_understanding_service.generate_understanding; understanding_storage.save_understanding persists the result.

runtime_flow.title: Build HTML documentation and save
runtime_flow.description: DocumentBuilder.build_html creates a standalone HTML doc from the stored understanding (and runtime_context if present). DocumentStorage.save_document writes the HTML and metadata and returns document metadata. The API responds with created or updated action depending on comparison results.
runtime_flow.evidence: _generate_and_save_document calls document_builder.build_html and document_storage.save_document; endpoints return DocumentationSyncResponse with action 'created', 'updated', or 'unchanged'.

runtime_flow.title: View or retrieve generated documentation
runtime_flow.description: Generated HTML can be downloaded or opened via GET /{project_name}/documents/{document_id}/html. The Streamlit UI constructs the document URL and opens it in browser.
runtime_flow.evidence: Route get_project_document_html exists and returns FileResponse, and ui.streamlit_app.build_document_url constructs the URL via normalize_api_url and quote.

screenshot_insights.title: Backend Uvicorn logs showing successful API calls
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Terminal output of 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' with the server running on http://127.0.0.1:8000 and several GET requests to /api/projects/AutoDocX/documents returning 200 OK.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Confirms the FastAPI backend is running locally and that the documents-listing endpoint is reachable and returning successful responses during testing. Matches the code's route for listing project documents (app.api.project_routes list_project_documents).

screenshot_insights.title: Streamlit UI running and deprecation warnings
screenshot_insights.what_it_shows: Streamlit started (Local URL http://localhost:8501, Network URL shown) and repeated warnings about the deprecated use_container_width parameter.
screenshot_insights.why_it_matters: Validates the Streamlit frontend is running and accessible and that the UI may need a minor update (replace use_container_width with width). Also indicates the UI is prepared to submit context blocks and screenshots to the backend (ui/streamlit_app.py functions exist).

operational_notes: Start backend: run 'uvicorn app.main:app --reload' (screenshot shows this exact command and server on 127.0.0.1:8000).
Start UI: run 'streamlit run ui/streamlit_app.py' (screenshot shows Streamlit server on http://localhost:8501).

Use the Streamlit UI to send context blocks + screenshots; it builds multipart/form-data with 'context_blocks_json' and 'screenshots' files. See ui.streamlit_app.build_context_blocks_payload and sync_documentation_with_context.

Configure LLM provider and keys in environment (.env): get_llm_provider()/get_llm_api_key()/get_llm_model() are used by the understanding generation flows. If these are not set or invalid, the API will raise ValueError (app.core.config).

Scans, understandings, and documents are persisted to workspace directories created by create_required_directories(); inspect workspace/scans/, workspace/understandings/, workspace/documents/ to debug stored artifacts.

Temporary uploaded screenshots are saved via NamedTemporaryFile and removed by _cleanup_temp_files after DocumentStorage.prepare_runtime_assets copies them. If the process fails before cleanup, temporary files may remain in the temp directory.
When calling the API from the UI, normalize the base API URL (ui.streamlit_app.normalize_api_url) so trailing slashes are removed and endpoint building is correct.
Streamlit UI emits deprecation warnings: update any use_container_width calls to the new width parameter as suggested by the log to avoid future breakage.

risks_or_gaps.title: Missing or misconfigured LLM credentials
risks_or_gaps.description: The code relies on environment variables for LLM provider, API key and (optionally) model. get_llm_provider and get_llm_api_key will raise ValueError when missing/invalid, causing endpoints that require LLM calls (understand_project, update_document, sync flows) to fail.

risks_or_gaps.title: Temporary files left on failure
risks_or_gaps.description: Uploaded screenshots are written to temporary files and then expected to be cleaned up by _cleanup_temp_files. If an exception is raised between saving temp files and cleanup (for example during LLM analysis or document save), temporary files can remain on disk.

risks_or_gaps.title: Privacy exposure of uploaded screenshots
risks_or_gaps.description: Screenshots are uploaded and stored as runtime assets and may contain sensitive information (credentials, PII, internal URLs). There is no mention in the scan metadata of automatic redaction or access controls; local filesystem storage may be inappropriate for sensitive content.

risks_or_gaps.title: Streamlit UI deprecation warnings
risks_or_gaps.description: The Streamlit app is using 'use_container_width' which is deprecated; although not critical, the UI will require an update before the attribute is removed to avoid runtime errors in future Streamlit releases.

risks_or_gaps.title: Local filesystem storage may limit scaling
risks_or_gaps.description: Scan, understanding, and document storage are implemented using local workspace directories. This is fine for local development but may be insufficient for production deployments that require durability, access control, or distributed storage.
